html:
[3442]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 泊舟望月 - Literal meaning 'moor the boat and gaze at the moon,' a poetic description of a serene moment.
  • 心異之 - Meaning 'found it strange' or 'was surprised by it.'
  • 命酒與歌宴 - Meaning 'ordered wine and a singing banquet,' indicating a lavish and joyful gathering.
  • 甚怅然 - Meaning 'greatly disappointed' or 'feeling regretful.'
  • 率騎攻 - Meaning 'led cavalry to attack.'
  • 掩襲 - Meaning 'suddenly attacked' or 'launched a surprise attack.'
  • 相迎笑 - Meaning 'greeted with smiles,' indicating a friendly and welcoming gesture.
  • 畫壁題 - Meaning 'inscribed on the painted wall,' referring to a written message or inscription on a wall.
  • 欲渡河 - Meaning 'wanted to cross the river.'
  • 用爾作波津 - Meaning 'use you as a ferryman' or 'employ you as a crossing point.'
  • 令當爲缩水脈 - Meaning 'ordered to shrink the water pulse' or 'instructed to make the river shallow,' indicating a supernatural or magical command.
  • 陷京 - Meaning 'captured the capital.'
  • 弑莊帝 - Meaning 'killed Emperor Zhuang,' referring to the assassination of the emperor.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 齊明帝 - Refers to Emperor Wu of Southern Qi, a ruler of the Southern Qi dynasty.
  • 建武 - Refers to the Jianwu era, a period during the reign of Emperor Wu.
  • 蕭岳 - A scholar or official during the Southern Qi dynasty.
  • 毘陵 - An ancient name for modern-day Wuxi, Jiangsu Province.
  • 延陵季子廟 - Refers to the temple dedicated to Ji Ziqi (季子), a famous figure from the Spring and Autumn period.
  • 爾朱兆 - A military leader during the Northern Wei dynasty.
  • 汾州 - An ancient name for a region in modern-day Shanxi Province.
  • 洛師 - Refers to Luoyang, the capital of the Northern Wei dynasty.
  • 河梁 - Refers to a bridge or crossing point over a river.
  • 京邑 - Refers to the capital city.
  • 莊帝 - Refers to Emperor Xiaoming of Northern Wei, also known as Emperor Zhuang.

Proper Nouns

  • 齊明帝 - Emperor Wu of Southern Qi.
  • 建武 - Jianwu era.
  • 蕭岳 - Flao Yue, a scholar or official.
  • 毘陵 - Wuxi, Jiangsu Province.
  • 延陵季子廟 - Ji Ziqi Temple.
  • 爾朱兆 - Erzhu Zhao, a military leader.
  • 汾州 - Fen Prefecture, Shanxi Province.
  • 洛師 - Luoyang, Northern Wei capital.
  • 河梁 - A river crossing.
  • 京邑 - Capital city.
  • 莊帝 - Emperor Zhuang of Northern Wei.

[3443]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 梁旱甚 - Indicates a severe drought in the state of Liang.
  • 诏于蒋帝神求雨 - The emperor issued an edict to pray for rain at the temple of Emperor Jiang.
  • 十旬不降 - For ten days (a旬 is ten days) there was no rain.
  • 帝怒 - The emperor became angry.
  • 尔日开朗 - On that day, the sky was clear.
  • 将欲起火 - When he was about to set fire.
  • 须臾骤雨 - Soon, heavy rain fell.
  • 台中宫殿皆自震动 - The imperial palace and all its buildings shook.
  • 帝惧驰诏追停 - The emperor was frightened and quickly issued an edict to stop the burning.
  • 少时还静 - After a short time, everything returned to calm.
  • 自此帝诚信遂深 - From then on, the emperor's faith and trust in the deity deepened.
  • 自践阼比未曾到庙 - Since ascending the throne, he had never visited the temple before.
  • 于是备法驾将朝臣修谒 - He then prepared his ceremonial chariot and led his officials to pay homage.
  • 蒋帝神报敕必许扶助 - The deity of Emperor Jiang promised to assist.
  • 遂大克魏军 - They then greatly defeated the Wei army.
  • 神之力也 - This was the power of the deity.
  • 凯旋之后庙中人马脚皆有泥湿 - After returning victorious, the statues of people and horses in the temple were all muddy and wet.
  • 临汝侯猷 - A member of the imperial clan, Lord You of Linru.
  • 宗室临汝侯猷为吴兴太守 - Lord You served as the governor of Wuxing.
  • 性倜傥 - He was bold and unrestrained.
  • 与楚庙神交饮至一斛 - He would drink with the deity of the Chu temple, consuming up to one dou (斛 is a measure of volume, roughly equivalent to 10 liters).
  • 所祷必应 - His prayers were always answered.
  • 后为益州刺史 - Later, he became the governor of Yizhou.
  • 时江陵人齐狗儿反众十余万攻州城 - At that time, a rebel named Qi Dog'er from Jiangling led tens of thousands of troops to attack the city.
  • 猷兵粮已尽人有二心 - Lord You's soldiers were running out of food and morale was low.
  • 乃遥祷请救 - He then prayed for help from afar.
  • 是日州界田父逢一骑络铁从东方来 - On that day, a farmer in the region met a rider with iron armor coming from the east.
  • 问去城防里曰百四十里 - The farmer asked how far it was to the city, and the rider said it was 140 li.
  • 日已晡骑语父曰后人来可令疾马欲及日破贼 - It was already late afternoon, and the rider told the farmer, 'If more people come, make them ride fast so we can break through the enemy by sunset.'
  • 俄有数百骑如风 - Soon, hundreds of riders came like the wind.
  • 一骑仍请饮田父问为谁曰吴兴楚王来救临汝侯 - One rider asked the farmer for a drink, and when asked who he was, he said, 'I am the King of Chu from Wuxing, here to rescue Lord You.'
  • 当此时庙中请祈无验 - At that time, no one could verify the prayer in the temple.
  • 十余日乃见侍卫土偶皆泥湿如汗者 - After more than ten days, the clay figures in the temple, representing attendants, were all muddy and wet, as if they had been sweating.
  • 是日猷大破狗儿焉 - On that day, Lord You decisively defeated Qi Dog'er.
  • 及猷卒谥曰灵 - When Lord You died, he was posthumously given the title of 'Ling' (灵).
  • 与神交故也 - Because of his close relationship with the deity.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 蒋帝神 - The deity of Emperor Jiang, a revered figure in the region.
  • 梁 - A historical state during the Southern and Northern Dynasties period.
  • 魏将杨大眼 - General Yang Dayan of the Northern Wei dynasty.
  • 钟离 - A place name, likely referring to Zhongli, a strategic location.
  • 临汝侯猷 - A nobleman of the Liang dynasty, known for his interactions with deities.
  • 吴兴太守 - The governor of Wuxing, a region in modern-day Zhejiang province.
  • 楚庙神 - The deity of the Chu temple, associated with Lord You.
  • 益州刺史 - The governor of Yizhou, a region in modern-day Sichuan province.
  • 江陵人齐狗儿 - A rebel leader from Jiangling, a place in modern-day Hubei province.
  • 谥曰灵 - The posthumous title 'Ling,' which means 'spirit' or 'soul,' often given to those who were spiritually connected to deities.

[3445]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 聘于梁 - To be sent as an envoy to Liang, indicating a diplomatic mission.
  • 忽闻水有香气异常 - A vivid, idiomatic expression describing the sudden and unusual occurrence of a sweet fragrance in the water.
  • 牛乃惊奔 - The ox was startled and ran off, a common phrase to describe sudden panic.
  • 曳车入河 - Dragged the carriage into the river, indicating the forceful action of the ox.
  • 兄子十住 - The nephew, ten years old, using a traditional way to denote age.
  • 尚幼 - Still young, emphasizing the youth of the nephew.
  • 太山君呼君爲录事 - The Lord of Tai Shan called you to be the recorder, a formal summons in the underworld.
  • 牒曰 - The document says, introducing a written record.
  • 董慎名称荗实 - Dong Shen has a name that is both grand and substantial, praising his reputation.
  • 案牍精练 - Documents are well-prepared and refined, highlighting his administrative skills.
  • 权差知右曹录事 - Temporarily appointed as the recorder of the Right Censorate, indicating a temporary promotion.
  • 倨慎谓事者曰 - Jue Shen said to the messenger, introducing a dialogue.
  • 府君呼我 - The Lord of the City called me, a formal address in the underworld.
  • 防司 - The guard, referring to the official responsible for security in the underworld.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 北齐 - Refers to the Northern Qi dynasty, one of the Northern dynasties during the period of division in China.
  • 梁 - Refers to the Liang dynasty, one of the Southern dynasties during the same period.
  • 太山君 - The Lord of Tai Shan, a deity in Chinese mythology associated with the underworld and judgment.
  • 录事 - A low-ranking official responsible for recording and managing documents.
  • 大业元年 - The first year of the Daye era, during the reign of Emperor Yang of Sui.
  • 兖州 - An ancient Chinese administrative region, roughly corresponding to modern-day Shandong province.
  • 隋 - Refers to the Sui dynasty, which unified China after the period of division.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The phrase '忽闻水有香气异常' is vivid but could be interpreted as either a sensory description or a supernatural event.
  • The sentence structure '太山君呼君爲录事' is elliptical, but it clearly indicates a formal summons.
  • The long sentence '董慎名称荗实，案牍精练，将平疑狱，须俟良能，权差知右曹录事' describes Dong Shen's qualifications and the reason for his appointment, requiring careful segmentation in English.
  • The dialogue marker '倨慎谓事者曰' introduces a conversation, which should be clearly separated in English.
  • The question-and-answer structure '府君呼我，岂有不行，然不识府君名谓何' should be preserved to maintain the flow of the dialogue.

Proper Nouns

  • 卢元明 - The name of the envoy from Northern Qi.
  • 董慎 - The name of the official from the Sui dynasty.
  • 太山君 - The name of the deity in the underworld.
  • 大业元年 - The specific year of the Daye era.
  • 兖州 - The name of the administrative region.

[3446]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 幽秘恐或漏泄 - Phrase meaning 'the secret might be leaked out,' indicating a concern for confidentiality.
  • 向请左曹匿影布囊盛之 - Phrase meaning 'previously, the Left Censor's shadow was hidden in a bag,' referring to a past incident involving secrecy.
  • 府君大笑曰已死 - Phrase meaning 'the Lord of the Mansion laughed and said it was already dead,' indicating a dismissive or humorous response.
  • 范慎追董慎取左曹囊盛右曹录事 - Phrase meaning 'Fan Shen pursued Dong Shen and took the bag containing the Right Censor's records,' describing a specific action taken by Fan Shen.
  • 可谓能防慎也 - Phrase meaning 'it can be said that he was able to prevent caution,' indicating a successful action.
  • 便令写出抉去目泥赐青缣衫鱼须笏豹皮靴 - Phrase meaning 'then ordered him to write out and remove the mud from his eyes, and granted him a blue silk robe, a fish whisker tablet, and leopard skin boots,' describing a series of actions and rewards.
  • 文甚斑駮 - Phrase meaning 'the writing was very patchy,' indicating the state of the document.
  • 邀登副阶命左右取榻令坐曰借君公正故有是请 - Phrase meaning 'invited him to ascend the secondary terrace and ordered the attendants to fetch a couch and make him sit, saying, 'I borrow your fairness, so I have this request,' indicating a formal and respectful interaction.
  • 今有闽州司马令狐实等六人寘无间狱承天曹符以实是太元夫人三等亲准令逓减三等 - Phrase meaning 'now there are six people, including Sima Lingfu from Minzhou, who are imprisoned in the death chamber. According to the decree from Heaven's Department, since Lingfu is a third-degree relative of the Lady Taoyuan, the punishment should be reduced by three degrees,' describing a legal and familial context.
  • 昨罪人程翥一百二十人引例喧讼不可止遏已具名申天曹 - Phrase meaning 'yesterday, a hundred and twenty criminals, led by Cheng Zhu, cited precedents and loudly protested, which could not be stopped. Their names have already been submitted to Heaven's Department,' indicating a protest and its consequences.
  • 天曹以爲罚疑唯轻亦令量减二等 - Phrase meaning 'Heaven's Department believes that in cases of doubt, the punishment should be lighter and has ordered a reduction of two degrees,' indicating a decision on punishment.
  • 余恐后人引例多矣君谓宜如何 - Phrase meaning 'I fear that future people will cite many precedents. What do you think we should do?,' indicating a concern about future legal interpretations.
  • 慎曰夫水照妍蚩而人不怨者以至清无情况于天地 - Phrase meaning 'Fan Shen said, 'Water reflects beauty and ugliness, but people do not resent it because it is so clear and impartial,' indicating a philosophical reflection.
  • 刑法岂宜恩贷奸慝 - Phrase meaning 'punishment should not be lenient towards evil-doers,' indicating a stance on justice.
  • 然愼一胥吏耳素无文字虽知不可终语无 - Phrase meaning 'However, Fan Shen is just a low-ranking official and has no formal education, so he knows it is not right but ultimately has no words,' indicating a self-deprecating statement.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 幽秘 - Refers to a secret or confidential matter.
  • 左曹 - Refers to the Left Censor, a high-ranking official in the imperial court.
  • 府君 - Refers to the Lord of the Mansion, a title for a high-ranking official or noble.
  • 范慎 - A historical figure known for his actions and decisions.
  • 董慎 - Another historical figure involved in the same incident.
  • 闽州 - A historical region in China, now part of Fujian Province.
  • 司马 - A military rank or position.
  • 令狐实 - A historical figure, likely a relative of the Lady Taoyuan.
  • 太元夫人 - A title for a noblewoman, possibly a relative of the emperor.
  • 承天曹 - A department in the imperial bureaucracy responsible for certain legal matters.
  • 程翥 - A historical figure who led a protest.
  • 天曹 - A department in the imperial bureaucracy, often associated with the heavens or divine authority.

Proper Nouns

  • 幽秘 - Secret or confidential matter.
  • 左曹 - Left Censor.
  • 府君 - Lord of the Mansion.
  • 范慎 - Historical figure.
  • 董慎 - Historical figure.
  • 闽州 - Historical region.
  • 司马 - Military rank.
  • 令狐实 - Historical figure.
  • 太元夫人 - Noblewoman.
  • 承天曹 - Department in the imperial bureaucracy.
  • 程翥 - Historical figure.
  • 天曹 - Department in the imperial bureaucracy.

[3447]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 条贯 - To organize or systematize, often used in the context of governance or administration.
  • 足得备君管记 - To be able to serve as an assistant or secretary to a high-ranking official.
  • 易耳 - Colloquial expression meaning 'easy' or 'simple'.
  • 补左曹录事 - '补' means 'to fill a vacancy,' and '左曹录事' refers to a specific administrative position.
  • 赐衣服如董慎 - '赐' means 'to bestow,' and '如董慎' refers to a specific type of clothing, likely named after a historical figure.
  • 天本无私法宜画一 - '天本无私' means 'Heaven is inherently impartial,' and '法宜画一' means 'laws should be uniform'.
  • 减刑已同私请 - '减刑' means 'to reduce punishment,' and '私请' means 'private request'.
  • 罪疑 - Refers to cases where guilt is uncertain.
  • 失公家之论 - '失' means 'to lose,' and '公家之论' means 'the public's opinion'.
  • 依前付无间录狱 - '依前' means 'as before,' and '无间录狱' refers to a specific legal procedure.
  • 天曹 - Refers to the heavenly bureaucracy or divine authority.
  • 黄衫人 - A term that can refer to a person in official or ceremonial attire, possibly a messenger or official.
  • 无不亲 - Refers to the idea that nothing is unconnected or unrelated, often used in the context of universal principles.
  • 元化匮中释冲符 - '元化' refers to the fundamental principles of the universe, '匮' means 'treasure,' and '释冲符' refers to a specific text or interpretation.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 秀才 - Refers to a scholar or student who has passed the lowest level of the imperial examinations.
  • 管记 - Refers to an assistant or secretary to a high-ranking official.
  • 左曹录事 - A specific administrative position in the imperial bureaucracy.
  • 周礼 - Refers to the *Rites of Zhou*, a classic text on rituals and governance.
  • 八议 - A legal principle in the *Rites of Zhou* that allows certain individuals to be exempt from certain punishments.
  • 三等之亲 - Refers to the three degrees of kinship in Chinese society, often used in legal or ritual contexts.

Proper Nouns

  • 张审通 - The name of the scholar being discussed.
  • 周礼 - The *Rites of Zhou*, a classic text on rituals and governance.
  • 八议 - A legal principle in the *Rites of Zhou*.
  • 三等之亲 - Refers to the three degrees of kinship in Chinese society.

[3448]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 愆違 - Refers to faults or transgressions, often in a legal or moral context.
  • 罰不衣紫 - A form of punishment where the individual is not allowed to wear purple, symbolizing a loss of status or privilege.
  • 六十甲子 - Refers to the sixty-year cycle of the Chinese calendar, often used to denote time or age.
  • 大怒 - Indicates intense anger or wrath.
  • 方寸肉 - A small piece of flesh, often used metaphorically to denote a minor punishment.
  • 塞其一耳 - Literally 'to plug one ear,' which could symbolize a form of punishment or a way to block out sound.
  • 逆耳之辜 - Refers to the guilt or offense of speaking words that are unpleasant to hear.
  • 沃心之藥 - Metaphorically means 'a medicine for the heart,' suggesting advice or counsel aimed at moral or spiritual improvement.
  • 太古 - Refers to ancient times, often idealized as a period of simplicity and purity.
  • 中古之降 - Indicates a decline from the ancient period, often associated with moral or cultural deterioration.
  • 各親 - Refers to the principle of favoring one's own relatives, often seen as a form of nepotism.
  • 平均 - Means 'equitable' or 'fair,' suggesting a desire for justice or balance.
  • 天符 - Refers to a divine or heavenly mandate, often used in the context of divine approval or intervention.
  • 依正法 - Means to follow the proper or correct method, often in a legal or moral context.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 府君 - A respectful title for a local official or magistrate, often used in the context of local governance and justice.
  • 判辞 - Refers to a written judgment or decision, often in the context of legal proceedings.
  • 黄衣人 - Refers to a person dressed in yellow, often associated with officials or messengers in Chinese culture.
  • 天曹 - The heavenly court, a concept in Chinese mythology and religion where divine judgment is carried out.
  • 太古 - The ancient past, often idealized in Chinese philosophy and literature as a time of simplicity and purity.
  • 中古之降 - The decline from the ancient period, often associated with moral or cultural deterioration.
  • 各親 - The principle of favoring one's own relatives, often seen as a form of nepotism.
  • 平均 - The concept of fairness or equity, often associated with justice and moral balance.

Proper Nouns

  • 府君 - A title for a local official or magistrate.
  • 审通 - The name of the character being punished and appealing for leniency.
  • 仲尼 - A respectful reference to Confucius, often used in the context of moral or philosophical teachings.

[3449]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 可加六天副正使令狐实程翥等并正法置处 - Elliptical phrase combining multiple actions: 'can be added,' 'six-day deputy chief envoy,' 'Linghu Shi Cheng Zhu and others,' 'and be dealt with according to law and placed in custody.'
  • 府君即谓审通曰非君不可正此狱 - Compact sentence structure: 'the official told Shen Tong that without you, this case cannot be properly handled.'
  • 因命左右割下耳中肉令一小儿擘之爲耳安于审通额上 - Elliptical sentence requiring careful parsing: 'then ordered the attendants to cut the flesh from his ear and had a child split it to form an ear and place it on Shen Tong's forehead.'
  • 塞君一耳与君三耳何如 - Elliptical sentence requiring careful parsing: 'to block one of your ears, how about giving you three ears instead?'
  • 甚頼君荐贤以成我羙然不可久留君当寿一周年相报耳 - Elliptical sentence requiring careful parsing: 'I greatly rely on you to recommend virtuous people to complete my beauty, but you cannot stay for too long; I will reward you with a year of life.'
  • 君兼本寿得二十一年矣 - Elliptical sentence requiring careful parsing: 'you, combined with your original lifespan, will live for twenty-one years.'
  • 欻如写出而顾问妻子 - Elliptical sentence requiring careful parsing: 'suddenly as if written out, to consult with one's wife and children.'
  • 妻子云君亡精魂已十余日矣 - Elliptical sentence requiring careful parsing: 'your wife and children said you had been dead for more than ten days.'
  • 慎自此果二十一年而卒 - Elliptical sentence requiring careful parsing: 'Shen indeed lived for twenty-one years and then died.'
  • 审通数日额觉痒遂踊出一耳通前三耳而踊出者尤聪 - Elliptical sentence requiring careful parsing: 'Shen Tong felt his forehead itching after several days and an ear popped out; the ear that popped out was particularly sharp-sighted.'
  • 时人笑曰天有九头鸟地有三耳秀才亦呼爲鸡冠秀才者慎初思府君称邻后方知倨乃邻字也 - Elliptical sentence requiring careful parsing: 'people laughed and said that there are nine-headed birds in the sky and three-eared scholars on the ground; also called Shen Chusi 'chicken crown scholar'; it was only after he was called 'neighbor' that he realized '倨' was a homophone for '邻' (neighbor).'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 府君 - Refers to a high-ranking official, often a local magistrate or governor.
  • 审通 - The name of a person involved in the story.
  • 慎 - The name of another person involved in the story.
  • 九头鸟 - A mythical bird with nine heads, often used in Chinese folklore to symbolize something extraordinary or unusual.
  • 三耳秀才 - A term used to describe someone with three ears, which is a rare and unusual physical characteristic.
  • 鸡冠秀才 - A term used to describe someone with a distinctive appearance, often associated with intelligence or wisdom.
  • 邻 - The character for 'neighbor,' which is also a homophone for '倨' (arrogant).

Proper Nouns

  • 府君 - A high-ranking official.
  • 审通 - A person involved in the story.
  • 慎 - Another person involved in the story.
  • 令狐实 - A person involved in the story.
  • 程翥 - A person involved in the story.
  • 九头鸟 - A mythical bird with nine heads.
  • 三耳秀才 - A term used to describe someone with three ears.
  • 鸡冠秀才 - A term used to describe someone with a distinctive appearance.
  • 邻 - The character for 'neighbor.'
  • 倨 - The character for 'arrogant.'

[3450]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 卫公李靖始困于贫贱 - 卫公李靖 is the title and name of the person, 始困于贫贱 means he was initially poor and of low social status.
  • 因过华山庙诉于神 - 因过 means because he passed by, 华山庙 is the name of the temple, 诉于神 means he pleaded with the god.
  • 且请告以官位所至 - 且请告 means and asked to be told, 以官位所至 means about the positions he would reach.
  • 辞色抗厉 - 辞色 means words and expressions, 抗厉 means firm and resolute.
  • 伫立良久乃出庙门百许步 - 伫立 means stood still, 良久 means for a long time, 乃出庙门 means then left the temple gate, 百许步 means about a hundred steps.
  • 闻后大声曰李仆射好去 - 闻后 means after hearing, 大声曰 means shouted loudly, 李仆射好去 means Goodbye, General Li.
  • 顾之不见人 - 顾之 means looked back, 不见人 means did not see anyone.
  • 后竟至端揆 - 后竟至 means later eventually reached, 端揆 means the highest position in the government.
  • 太平广记卷二百九十六 - 太平广记 is a collection of stories, 卷二百九十六 means Volume 296.
  • 钦定四库全书 - 钦定四库全书 is an imperial collection of books.
  • 神七 - 神七 means God Seven or Divine Seven.
  • 丹丘子 - 丹丘子 is a name or title of a person.
  • 瀚海神 - 瀚海神 means God of the Great Sea.
  • 薛延陀 - 薛延陀 is a name or title of a person.
  • 睦仁蒨 - 睦仁蒨 is a name or title of a person.

  • This sentence describes Zhi Gan's arrival, his communication with the governor, the investigation, and the discovery that the official had died at the same time as the Ministry's announcement.
  • This sentence describes Zhi Gan's mission, the escape of the prisoners, his distress, and the appearance of a former subordinate who reported the prisoners' capture.
  • This sentence describes the fate of the prisoners and the subordinate's departure.
  • This sentence describes Zhi Gan's verification of the subordinate's report and the outcome of the confrontation.
  • This sentence provides Zhi Gan's current position, the testimonies of Liu Xing and Pei Tongjie, and their corroborative statements.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 累举不第天宝初乃以琴酒为事: After failing repeatedly in the imperial examinations, in the early years of the Tianbao era, he devoted himself to music and wine.
  • 凡饮皆敬酬之恒游东市: He treated everyone who drank with him respectfully and always roamed the eastern market.
  • 遇友人饮于酒肆一日偶值一举人相得甚欢乃邀与之饮: One day, while meeting friends at a wine shop, he coincidentally met a scholar and they got along very well, so he invited him to drink.
  • 庭训曰君未饮何醉也曰吾非人乃华原梓桐神也: Wei Tingxun said, 'Why are you drunk if you haven't even drunk?' The god replied, 'I am not a person, I am the deity of Huayuan's zelkova and sycamore trees.'
  • 言讫而去后旬日乃访之至庙神已令二使迎庭训入庙: After finishing speaking, he left. Ten days later, he visited the temple, and the god had already sent two messengers to welcome Wei Tingxun into the temple.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 能受此术乎 - Direct question meaning 'Can you accept this technique?'
  • 空有名未之覩也 - Literal meaning 'only known by name, never seen'; conveys skepticism about the effectiveness of the technique
  • 徒闻秦始汉武好此道而终无成 - Literal meaning 'only heard that Qin Shi Huang and Han Wudi liked this path but ultimately achieved nothing'; alludes to the historical failure of these emperors in their pursuit of immortality
  • 祗為千载讥诮耳 - Literal meaning 'only to be mocked for a thousand years'; expresses disdain for their efforts
  • 神人曰 - Literal meaning 'The divine being said'; introduces a supernatural figure who possesses knowledge of the technique
  • 昔秦皇汉武帝王也 - Literal meaning 'In the past, Qin Shi Huang and Han Wudi were emperors'; establishes their historical status
  • 处救人之位自有救人之术 - Literal meaning 'In the position of saving others, there is naturally a method to save others'; contrasts their actual duties with their misguided pursuits
  • 而不行反求神仙之术则非也 - Literal meaning 'But they did not follow this and instead sought immortality, which is wrong'; critiques their actions
  • 尔无救人之位而欲救天下之人固可行此术 - Literal meaning 'You do not have the position to save others, but if you wish to save the people of the world, you can certainly practice this technique'; justifies the technique for the protagonist
  • 常曰黄金成水银化真有之乎 - Literal meaning 'Chang said, 'Is it truly true that gold can turn into mercury?''; introduces a key alchemical concept
  • 神人曰尔勿疑夫黄金生于山石其始乃山石之精液千年为水银水银受太阴之气固流荡而不凝定微偶纯阳之气合则化黄金于倐忽也 - Literal meaning 'The divine being said, 'Do not doubt. Gold originates from mountains and stones, and its beginning is the essence of the rocks. After a thousand years, it becomes mercury. Mercury receives the Yin energy of the moon, so it flows and does not settle. But if it encounters the Yang energy, it instantly turns into gold.''; provides a detailed explanation of alchemical theory
  • 今若以水银欲化黄金不必须在山即化不在山即不化但偶纯阳之气合即化矣 - Literal meaning 'Now, if you want to turn mercury into gold, it does not have to be done in a mountain. It will turn into gold if the Yang energy is present, but not if it is absent.'; explains the practical application of the theory

  • 黄金成水银化 - Refers to the alchemical process of turning gold into mercury and vice versa, a key concept in Chinese alchemy
  • 山神 - Refers to a mountain spirit, a common figure in Chinese folklore and mythology, often associated with hidden knowledge and treasures

  • 厄皆先受数杖二十以上皆死二十以下但重病耳 - Elliptical phrase meaning 'those who are in distress first receive several beatings, those over twenty will die, and those under twenty will only suffer severe illness.'
  • 以此斟酌往往误言于里中未尝差也 - Complex statement meaning 'based on this consideration, they often misreport, but never miss the mark.'
  • 公慎不可泄露邻县令某是也 - Warning and revelation meaning 'you must be cautious, do not reveal it, the magistrate of the neighboring county is the one.'
  • 闻即当来此公自求之必不可言得之于某旬日邻宰果来与畅俱诣州季集畅淩晨逺迎馆于县宅燕劳加等 - Long sequence of events meaning 'upon hearing, he will come here, you must seek it out yourself, but do not say it came from me, after ten days, the neighboring magistrate indeed came, accompanied by Chang, they went to the state capital to meet with the governor, Chang welcomed them early in the morning at the county residence, and he treated them with great hospitality.'
  • 既至乃一老翁七十余矣 - Simple statement meaning 'upon arrival, it turned out to be an elderly man in his seventies.'

  • 词色感愧乃与之俱诣郡又与同归馆于县宅益为欢洽 - Description of the interaction and outcome meaning 'his words and expression were filled with gratitude and shame, so he went with them to the prefecture, and then accompanied them back to the county residence, and they became even more friendly.'
  • 明日将别其延于深室具簪笏再拜如问伍伯之词而加恳切 - Description of a formal meeting meaning 'the next day, when they were about to part, he invited them into a secluded room, prepared their official attire, bowed twice, like asking the gatekeeper's words, and added earnestness.'
  • 老宰厉声曰 - Simple statement meaning 'the old magistrate spoke sternly.'
  • 是谁言词色甚怒曰不白所言人终不为公言也如是 - Statement of threat and warning meaning 'who said this, his expression was very angry, if you do not reveal who told you, I will not speak for you, like this.'

  • The lengthy sentence '一日伺其将出佩即潜往窥之见乘马出延兴门马行空中佩惊问行者皆不见佩又随至城东墓田中巫者陈设酒肴沥酒祭地即见妇人下马就接而饮之其女僮随后收拾钱载于马上即变为铜钱又见妇人以防画地巫者随指其处曰此可以为穴事毕即乘马而回佩心甚恶之归具告母母曰吾固知是妖异为之' describes the husband's surveillance, the discovery of the wife's supernatural activities, and the mother's confirmation of her suspicions.

  • 须诏屏翳收风冯夷息浪 - A formal command to 'order the wind god to stop the wind and the river god to calm the waves,' invoking divine intervention.
  • 斯亦上帝素命礼宜躬亲 - Means 'this is also a command from the supreme deity, and it is proper to perform the rites personally,' emphasizing the importance of personal involvement in religious duties.
  • 吾子清尘得免举罚否 - A question asking 'will you be spared from punishment by avoiding the dust,' suggesting a plea for divine mercy.
  • 窃于水濵拉得范相国来 - Means 'I secretly pulled the former Prime Minister Fan to the water's edge,' indicating an informal and possibly unauthorized action.
  • 足以补其尤矣 - Means 'it is enough to make up for his shortcomings,' suggesting that the action helps to compensate for any deficiencies.
  • 披褐者仗劒而前 - A description of someone dressed in coarse clothing holding a sword and approaching, possibly indicating a humble or unconventional figure.
  • 溪湖神曰钦奉实久 - The lake and stream spirits say 'we have been serving for a long time,' indicating their long-standing role.
  • 吴人怀恩立祠于江濆 - Means 'the people of Wu, grateful for his kindness, built a temple by the riverbank,' indicating a place of worship.
  • 春秋设薄祀为村醪所困 - Means 'they held simple offerings in spring and autumn, but were troubled by village wine,' suggesting a limitation in resources.
  • 遂为江公驱来唐突盛筵 - Means 'they were driven here by the river god, rudely interrupting the feast,' indicating an unexpected and disruptive arrival.
  • 益增慙栗 - Means 'increasingly filled with shame and fear,' expressing feelings of embarrassment and anxiety.
  • 于是揖让入门既即席 - Means 'they then bowed and entered, taking their seats,' describing a formal and respectful entry.
  • 老蛟前唱曰湘王至 - An old dragon sings 'the King of Xiang has arrived,' indicating a significant and revered figure.
  • 去城二里俄闻骈阗车马声 - Means 'two miles from the city, suddenly heard the sound of many carriages and horses,' suggesting a grand and noisy procession.
  • 緑衣冠者气貌甚伟 - A person dressed in green and wearing a black hat, with a very imposing appearance, indicating a dignified and powerful figure.
  • 驱殿亦百余 - Means 'leading a retinue of more than a hundred,' indicating a large and impressive entourage.
  • 适辄与汨罗屈副使同来 - Means 'just now, he came with the Deputy Magistrate of Miluo Qu,' indicating a companion or associate.
  • 服饰与容貌惨悴者伛偻而进 - A person dressed in shabby clothes and with a haggard appearance, bowing and approaching, indicating a humble and possibly suffering figure.
  • 方即席范相笑谓屈原曰 - Fan smiles and says to Qu Yuan, 'the exiled minister, suffering from the waves and slander, his bones have not yet melted away, why such a sad face, further hunting for cups,' indicating a lighthearted and possibly teasing tone.
  • 被放逐之臣负波涛之困谗痕谤迹骨销未灭 - Means 'the exiled minister, suffering from the waves and slander, his bones have not yet melted away,' indicating a long-lasting and painful experience of exile and slander.
  • 何惨靣目更猎其杯 - Means 'why such a sad face, further hunting for cups,' suggesting a continuation of the teasing and possibly a call to enjoy life despite hardships.

  • 焉敢将喉舌酬对相国乎 - A rhetorical question expressing that it is not possible or appropriate to offer one's voice or words in response to a high-ranking official (相国).
  • 穿七札之箭不射笼中之鸟 - A metaphorical idiom meaning that a powerful arrow (symbolizing a strong action or force) should not be used against a bird in a cage (symbolizing a weak or helpless target).
  • 足下亡吴霸越 - Refers to the historical figure of Fan Li, who helped Goujian of Yue defeat King Fuchai of Wu and then retired.
  • 功成身退 - A common phrase meaning 'to withdraw after achieving success.'
  • 逍遥于五湖之上 - Refers to the idyllic life of living freely and leisurely, often associated with Fan Li's retirement to the Five Lakes region.
  • 辉焕于万古之后 - Means 'to shine brightly through all time,' suggesting lasting fame and glory.
  • 鄙夫窃仰重德盛名 - A humble expression meaning 'I, a lowly person, secretly admire your great virtue and fame.'
  • 不敢以常意奉待 - Means 'I dare not treat you with ordinary or casual respect.'
  • 何今日戯谑于绮席恃意气于放臣 - A rhetorical question expressing that it is inappropriate to jest with someone in a luxurious setting while treating them as a disgraced official.
  • 则何异射病鸟于笼中剸腐肉于几上 - Another rhetorical question, comparing the action to shooting a sick bird in a cage or cutting rotten meat on a table, both of which are seen as cruel or unnecessary.
  • 窃于君子惜金镞与利刃也 - A humble expression meaning 'I, a gentleman, regret the waste of precious arrows and sharp swords.'

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 穿七札之箭不射笼中之鸟刜洪钟之剑不剸几上之肉 - This sentence uses two parallel metaphors to illustrate the idea of using powerful tools or forces inappropriately.
  • 足下亡吴霸越功成身退逍遥于五湖之上辉焕于万古之后 - This sentence describes the historical figure of Fan Li, who achieved great success and then retired to a peaceful life.
  • 鄙夫窃仰重德盛名不敢以常意奉待 - This sentence expresses the speaker's admiration for the other person's virtue and fame, and the speaker's desire to treat them with respect.
  • 何今日戯谑于绮席恃意气于放臣 - This sentence is a rhetorical question expressing that it is inappropriate to jest with someone in a luxurious setting while treating them as a disgraced official.
  • 则何异射病鸟于笼中剸腐肉于几上 - This sentence is another rhetorical question, comparing the action to shooting a sick bird in a cage or cutting rotten meat on a table.
  • 窃于君子惜金镞与利刃也 - This sentence expresses the speaker's regret at the waste of precious resources.
  • 于是湘神动色命酒罚范君 - This sentence describes the reaction of the Xiang River deity, who becomes angry and orders punishment for Fan Li.
  • 将饮有女乐数十辈皆执所习于舞筵 - This sentence describes a scene where dozens of female entertainers are present to perform.
  • 俳优扬言曰皤皤美女唱公无渡河 - This sentence describes a jester or comedian who speaks out loudly, and an elderly beautiful woman who sings the song 'The Old Man Sang 'Do Not Cross the River.'

  • 白露溥兮西风高 - '白露溥' describes the widespread dew, and '西风高' refers to the strong western wind, evoking a seasonal atmosphere.
  • 碧波万里兮翻洪涛 - '碧波万里' describes vast green waves, and '翻洪涛' refers to the surging of great waves.
  • 莫言天下至柔者载舟覆舟皆我曹 - '莫言' means 'do not say,' '天下至柔者' refers to water, and '载舟覆舟皆我曹' is a metaphor for the power of water to both support and destroy, with '我曹' meaning 'we.'
  • 君不见夜来渡口拥千艘中载万姓之脂膏 - '君不见' means 'you do not see,' '夜来渡口拥千艘' describes a crowded river crossing at night, and '中载万姓之脂膏' refers to the wealth carried by the people.
  • 当楼船泛泛于叠浪恨珠贝又轻于鸿毛 - '当楼船泛泛于叠浪' describes a large ship floating on waves, and '恨珠贝又轻于鸿毛' expresses the frustration that precious things are as light as feathers.
  • 又不见潮来津亭维一舠中有一士青其袍赴宰邑之良日任波吼而风号是知溺名溺利者不免为水府之腥臊 - '又不见' means 'you do not see,' '潮来津亭维一舠' describes a single boat tied to a pier during high tide, and '中有一士青其袍赴宰邑之良日任波吼而风号' refers to a scholar in green robes facing a day of official duties amidst turbulent waves and howling winds. '是知溺名溺利者不免为水府之腥臊' is a conclusion that those obsessed with fame and profit will inevitably suffer in the depths of the water.

  • 酒至溪神湘王歌曰 - '酒至' means 'when the wine arrives,' and '溪神湘王歌曰' refers to the god of the stream and the King of Xiang singing.
  • 渺渺烟波接九嶷防人经此泣江蓠年年绿水青山色不改重华南狩时 - '渺渺烟波接九嶷' describes distant misty waves reaching the Nine Peaks, '防人经此泣江蓠' refers to people crying upon passing by the river, and '年年绿水青山色不改重华南狩时' suggests that the green waters and green mountains remain unchanged, while the emperor's southern hunting expeditions continue.

  • 我名永浮碧波虽涸兮 - 我名 means my name, 永浮 means to always float, 碧波 refers to green waves, 虽涸 means even if dried up. This phrase suggests that one's name will always be remembered, even if the waters dry up.
  • 厥誉长流向使甘言顺行于曩昔 - 厥誉 means their reputation, 长流 means to flow long, 向使 means if, 甘言 means sweet words, 顺行 means to follow the path, 曩昔 means the past. This phrase suggests that if sweet words had been followed in the past, the reputation would have flowed long.
  • 岂今日居君王之座头是知贪名徇禄而随世磨灭者 - 岂 means how, 今日 means today, 居 means to sit, 君王之座头 means the seat of the king, 是知 means to know, 贪名徇禄 means to seek fame and禄, 随世磨灭 means to be worn away by the world. This phrase questions how those who seek fame and禄 and follow the world will be remembered today.
  • 虽正寝之死乎无得与吾俦 - 虽 means even if, 正寝 means to die in one's bed, 无得 means not to be able, 与吾俦 means to be with me. This phrase suggests that even if one dies in their bed, they cannot be with the speaker.
  • 当鼎足之嘉会兮获周旋于君侯 - 当 means during, 鼎足 means three-way balance, 嘉会 means a good meeting, 获 means to obtain, 周旋 means to interact, 君侯 means a noble. This phrase describes a good meeting during a three-way balance, where one interacts with a noble.
  • 雕盘玉豆兮罗珍羞 - 雕盘 means carved plates, 玉豆 means jade dishes, 罗 means to arrange, 珍羞 means delicacies. This phrase describes the arrangement of delicacies on carved plates and jade dishes.
  • 金巵琼斚兮方献酬 - 金巵 means golden cups, 琼斚 means jade cups, 方 means just, 献酬 means to offer and return. This phrase describes the offering and returning of drinks in golden and jade cups.
  • 敢写心兮歌一曲 - 敢 means dare, 写心 means to express one's heart, 歌一曲 means to sing a song. This phrase suggests that one dares to express their heart by singing a song.
  • 无诮余持杯以淹留 - 无诮 means do not mock, 余 means I, 持杯 means to hold a cup, 以淹留 means to linger. This phrase suggests that one should not mock the speaker for holding a cup and lingering.
  • 申屠先生献境会夜宴诗曰 - 申屠先生 is a proper name, 献境会 means to offer a scene, 夜宴 means a night banquet, 诗曰 means the poem says. This phrase introduces the poem by a man named Shentu.
  • 行殿秋未晚水宫风初凉 - 行殿 means a traveling palace, 秋未晚 means autumn is not late, 水宫 means a water palace, 风初凉 means the wind is just cool. This phrase describes a traveling palace in autumn, where the wind is just cool.
  • 谁言此中夜得接朝宗行 - 谁言 means who says, 此中夜 means this middle night, 得接 means to be able to meet, 朝宗行 means the journey of the court. This phrase questions who says that one can meet the court's journey in the middle of the night.
  • 灵鼍振神龙耀煌煌 - 灵鼍 means a mythical creature, 振 means to shake, 神龙 means a divine dragon, 耀煌煌 means to shine brightly. This phrase describes a mythical creature shaking and a divine dragon shining brightly.
  • 红楼压波起翠幄连云张 - 红楼 means a red building, 压波 means to press the waves, 翠幄 means green canopies, 连云张 means to spread like clouds. This phrase describes a red building pressing the waves and green canopies spreading like clouds.
  • 玊箫冷吟秋瑶瑟清含商 - 玊箫 means a jade flute, 冷吟 means to coldly recite, 秋 means autumn, 瑶瑟 means a jade zither, 清含商 means to clearly contain the shang mode. This phrase describes a jade flute coldly reciting in autumn and a jade zither clearly containing the shang mode.
  • 贤臻江湖叟贵列川渎王 - 贤臻 means wise people come, 江湖叟 means old men of the rivers and lakes, 贵列 means to be listed among the noble, 川渎王 means the king of rivers and lakes. This phrase describes wise people coming to be listed among the noble as the king of rivers and lakes.
  • 谅予衰俗人无能振頽纲 - 谅予 means I believe, 衰俗人 means a declining person of the world, 无能 means unable, 振頽纲 means to revive the declining principles. This phrase suggests that the speaker believes they are unable to revive the declining principles.
  • 分辞皆乱世乐寐蛟螭乡 - 分辞 means divided words, 皆 means all, 乱世 means chaotic times, 乐寐 means to dream happily, 蛟螭乡 means the land of dragons and chimeras. This phrase suggests that all divided words are in chaotic times, dreaming happily in the land of dragons and chimeras.
  • 栖迟幽岛间防见波成桑 - 栖迟 means to linger, 幽岛 means a secluded island, 防见 means to prevent seeing, 波成桑 means waves turning into mulberry trees. This phrase describes lingering on a secluded island, preventing seeing waves turning into mulberry trees.
  • 尔来尽流俗难与倾壶觞 - 尔来 means since then, 尽 means all, 流俗 means the common people, 难与 means difficult to, 倾壶觞 means to empty the cups. This phrase suggests that since then, all common people are difficult to empty the cups.

  • 縁已被虚名 - Means 'because he has already been given a false reputation,' suggesting that the person is burdened by undeserved fame.
  • 不能下就小职 - Means 'unable to accept a minor position,' indicating reluctance to take on a lower-ranking role.
  • 遂至今空窃假王之号 - Means 'so he has continued to falsely hold the title of 'false king,' implying that the person has been using a title without legitimate authority.
  • 思少从容 - Means 'thinking to take some time to be at ease,' suggesting a desire for relaxation or leisure.
  • 幸一顾之惠 - Means 'graciously bestowing a glance of favor,' expressing gratitude for someone's attention or kindness.
  • 不知何以奉教 - Means 'do not know how to serve or instruct,' indicating humility and a desire to learn.
  • 本縁奉慕 - Means 'originally because of admiration,' explaining the reason for the encounter.
  • 顾展风仪 - Means 'to appreciate one's demeanor and bearing,' suggesting admiration for someone's appearance or character.
  • 何幸遽垂厚意 - Means 'how fortunate to receive such deep favor,' expressing gratitude for someone's kindness.
  • 诚有事则又如何 - Means 'if there is something to be done, then what should be done?' indicating a request for guidance on a matter.
  • 幸甚 - An exclamation of great happiness or relief.
  • 久闲散 - Means 'having been idle for a long time,' indicating a period of inactivity.
  • 思有以効用 - Means 'thinking of a way to be useful,' expressing a desire to contribute or serve.
  • 如今士马处处有主 - Means 'now soldiers and horses are owned by everyone,' indicating that resources are already claimed by others.
  • 不可夺他权柄 - Means 'cannot take away their authority,' suggesting that it is difficult to seize control from others.
  • 此后三年兴元当有八百人无主健儿 - Means 'in three years, in Xingyuan, there will be eight hundred unaffiliated strong men,' predicting a future situation.
  • 若早图谋必可将领 - Means 'if planned early, they can certainly be led,' suggesting that early planning will lead to success.
  • 所必奉托者 - Means 'those who must be entrusted,' indicating the people who will be relied upon.
  • 可致钱万张 - Means 'can obtain ten thousand sheets of paper money,' suggesting the acquisition of wealth.
  • 以此借手方谐矣 - Means 'only by using this method can it be harmonized,' indicating that a specific method will resolve a situation.
  • 许诺而寤 - Means 'promised and then woke up,' indicating a dream or vision.
  • 流汗霡霂 - Means 'sweating profusely,' describing a physical reaction.
  • 乃市万张以焚之 - Means 'then bought ten thousand sheets of paper money and burned them,' describing an action taken.
  • 及太和四年兴元节度使李绛遇害 - Means 'in the fourth year of Taihe, the Military Governor of Xingyuan, Li Jiang, was killed,' indicating a historical event.
  • 后节度使温造诛其凶党八百人 - Means 'later, the Military Governor Wen Zao executed eight hundred of the wicked followers,' describing a subsequent action.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 兴元 - Refers to Xingyuan, a region in ancient China.
  • 太和四年 - Refers to the fourth year of the Taihe era, a period during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 李绛 - Refers to Li Jiang, a historical figure who served as the Military Governor of Xingyuan.
  • 温造 - Refers to Wen Zao, another historical figure who succeeded Li Jiang as the Military Governor of Xingyuan.
  • 八百人无主健儿 - Refers to eight hundred strong men without a leader, indicating a group of warriors who were not affiliated with any faction.

Proper Nouns

  • 温造 - The name of another historical figure who succeeded Li Jiang as the Military Governor of Xingyuan.

  • 时朝以罂缶汲水引重之际泥滑颠仆于地地中素有折刀朝心正贯其刃久而士俊惧其未囘告于同幕者及到则已絶矣 - The official was drawing water with a gourd and pot, slipped on the mud, and fell to the ground. There was a broken knife in the ground, and his heart was pierced by its blade. He was dead when he returned.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 提三尺劒灭暴秦翦强楚而不能免其母乌老之称徒歌大风起兮云飞扬曷能威加四海哉 - Raising a three-foot sword, he destroyed the tyrannical Qin and cut down the powerful Chu, yet he could not escape the title of 'old man' and only sang 'The wind rises, clouds fly, how can he extend his power over the four seas?'
  • 徘徊庭庑间肆目久之乃还所止是夕才寐而卒见十数骑擒至庙庭 - He wandered around the temple for a long time, then returned to where he was staying. That night, as he was about to sleep, he saw more than ten riders capture him and take him to the temple courtyard.

  • 方及阶顾王生曰 - Formal statement meaning 'As he reached the steps, he looked back at Wang Sheng and said.'
  • 斯何人而见辱之甚也 - Formal question meaning 'What kind of person is this who has been so greatly insulted?'
  • 汉祖降阶对曰 - Formal statement meaning 'Emperor Han descended the steps and replied.'
  • 此虚妄侮慢之人也 - Formal accusation meaning 'This is a person who is false and disrespectful.'
  • 罪当斩之 - Formal judgment meaning 'The crime is punishable by death.'
  • 王生逞目太公遂厉声而言曰 - Formal statement meaning 'Wang Sheng glared, and the Grandfather spoke sternly and said.'
  • 臣览史籍见侮慢其君亲者尚无所贬 - Formal statement meaning 'I have read the history books and found that those who disrespect their emperor and kin are not criticized.'
  • 而贱臣戱语于神庙岂期肆于市朝哉 - Rhetorical question meaning 'Why should a low-ranking official's jest in a temple be spread in the marketplace?'
  • 在典册岂载侮慢君亲者当试征之 - Formal challenge meaning 'Is it recorded in the classics that those who disrespect their emperor and kin should be punished?'
  • 臣敢征大王可乎 - Formal question meaning 'May I present evidence to the Great King?'
  • 然 - Formal affirmation meaning 'Yes.'
  • 王即位会羣臣置酒前殿献太上皇寿有之乎 - Formal question meaning 'When the Great King ascended the throne, did he gather the ministers, hold a banquet in the front hall, and present a toast to the Emperor Emeritus?'
  • 既献寿乃曰 - Formal statement meaning 'After presenting the toast, he said.'
  • 大人常以臣无赖不事产业不如仲力 - Formal statement meaning 'The elder often said that I was a good-for-nothing, did not engage in business, and was not as capable as Zhong (Liu Bang's brother).'

  • 是侮慢其君亲矣 - Formal statement meaning 'This is an act of disrespect to the emperor and kin.'
  • 太公曰 - Formal statement meaning 'The Grandfather said.'
  • 汉祖黙然良久曰 - Formal statement meaning 'Emperor Han remained silent for a long time and said.'
  • 斩此物汚我三尺刃令搦髪者掴之一掴惘然而苏东方明矣以镜视腮有若指踪数日方灭 - Formal statement meaning 'Cut this thing with my three-foot sword and let the one holding the hair slap him once. He woke up dazed and confused, and by morning, he saw marks on his cheek like fingerprints, which took several days to fade.'

  • 夫如是与无为之道逺矣 - A philosophical statement suggesting that such actions are far removed from the path of non-action (无为).
  • 朕泣者怨已之命不合于父母而诉于旻天也 - The emperor laments that his fate does not align with his parents' wishes and he appeals to the sky (旻天).
  • 何万章之问孟轲不知其对传圣人之意岂宜如是乎 - Questions the appropriateness of Wan Zhang's questioning of Mencius, who did not know the answer, in conveying the meaning of the sages.
  • 夫莫之为而为之者天也莫之致而致之者命也 - Philosophical statements about fate (命) and heaven (天), suggesting that things happen without deliberate action but are guided by fate.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 悲风及三峡流泉 - Poetic phrase meaning 'the sorrowful wind and the flowing springs of the Three Gorges,' often used to evoke a melancholic atmosphere.
  • 未尝不尽夕而止 - Literary expression meaning 'never ceasing until nightfall,' indicating the continuous nature of the wind and springs.
  • 适闻君琴韵清雅 - Phrase meaning 'just now I heard the noble and elegant sound of your zither,' highlighting the beauty of the music.
  • 愿一听之 - Polite expression meaning 'I wish to listen to it,' showing respect and interest.
  • 旷乃弹别操 - Phrase meaning 'Kuang then played another piece,' indicating a change in the music.

  • 长叹曰 - Phrase meaning 'she sighed deeply and said,' indicating a moment of deep emotion.

  • 陈思王洛神赋如何 - Question meaning 'How is the *Luo Shen Fu* (赋 to the Goddess of the Luo River) by Chen Shigong?'
  • 真体物浏湸 - Phrase meaning 'truly vivid and lifelike,' praising the literary quality of the work.
  • 为梁昭明之精选尔 - Phrase meaning 'selected by Liang Zhaoming (Prince of Liang),' indicating the work's high status.
  • 女防笑曰 - Phrase meaning 'the woman laughed and said,' indicating a moment of humor.
  • 状妾之举止云翩若惊鸿婉若游龙 - Phrase meaning 'describing my demeanor as graceful as a startled swan and elegant as a winding dragon,' referring to a literary description.
  • 得无疎矣 - Phrase meaning 'isn't that a bit exaggerated?' indicating a critique of the description.
  • 陈思王之精魄今何在 - Question meaning 'Where is the spirit of Chen Shigong now?'
  • 见为遮须国王 - Phrase meaning 'he is now the king of Zhuxu (a kingdom),' indicating a transformation.
  • 何为遮须国 - Question meaning 'What is the kingdom of Zhuxu?'
  • 刘聪子死而复生 - Phrase meaning 'Liu Cong's son died and was reborn,' referring to a mythological event.
  • 有人告某云遮须国久无主待汝父来作主即此国是也 - Phrase meaning 'someone told me that the kingdom of Zhuxu has long been without a ruler, waiting for your father to come and take charge, and this is that kingdom,' indicating a divine message.
  • 俄有一青衣引一女曰织绡娘子至矣 - Phrase meaning 'soon a maid in green led a woman named Zexiao Lady to us,' indicating the arrival of a character.
  • 近日人世或传柳毅灵姻之事有之乎 - Question meaning 'Recently, there has been talk in the world about the miraculous marriage of Liu Yi, is there any truth to it?'

  • 刘聪 - Refers to Liu Cong (300-330 AD), a ruler of the Han Zhao kingdom during the Sixteen Kingdoms period.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 十得其四五尔余皆饰词不可惑也 - Out of ten, only four or five are true; the rest are embellished words and cannot be misleading.
  • 或闻龙畏铁有之乎 - A question asking if it is true that dragons fear iron.
  • 龙之神化虽铁石金玉尽可透达何独畏铁乎 - Even though dragons can transform and penetrate iron, stone, gold, and jade, why should they fear iron alone?
  • 畏者蛟螭辈也 - Those who fear are the lesser dragons and water serpents.
  • 雷氏子佩丰城劒至延平津跃入水化为龙有之乎 - A question asking if it is true that the son of Lei (a legendary figure) wore the sword from Fencheng and leaped into the water at Yanping, transforming into a dragon.
  • 妄也 - It is false or a lie.
  • 龙木劒乃金金既尅木而不相生焉能变化岂同雀入水为蛤野鸡入水为蜃哉 - A dragon is made of wood, while a sword is made of metal. Metal overcomes wood, so how can they transform? It is not like a sparrow turning into a clam or a wild chicken turning into a sea dragon.
  • 但宝劒灵物金水相生而入水雷生自不能沉于泉信其下搜劒不获乃妄言为龙且雷焕只言化去张司空但言俱合终不説为龙任劒之灵异且人之鼓铸锻链非自然之物是知终不能为龙明矣 - Although the sword is a precious and magical object, metal and water are mutually generating, and when it enters the water, it naturally does not sink. They searched for the sword below but did not find it, so they falsely claimed it turned into a dragon. Lei Huan only said it transformed, and Zhang Sike (a historical figure) only said they were together, never mentioning it turned into a dragon. The sword's magical nature and human craftsmanship mean it cannot truly transform into a dragon.
  • 梭化为龙如何 - How a shuttle (a tool used in weaving) can transform into a dragon.
  • 梭木也龙本属木变化归木又何怪也 - The shuttle is made of wood, and dragons are originally made of wood. If they transform back into wood, what is there to be surprised about?

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 雷氏子佩丰城劒至延平津跃入水化为龙有之乎 - Refers to a legendary story involving Lei Huan, a historical figure known for his expertise in swords. The story involves a sword from Fencheng and a transformation into a dragon.
  • 张司空 - Zhang Sike was a historical figure, possibly a high-ranking official or scholar, mentioned in the context of the sword transformation story.
  • 丰城劒 - The Fencheng sword is a legendary sword, often associated with Lei Huan and his expertise in swords.
  • 延平津 - This is a specific location, possibly a river or a place where the transformation is said to have occurred.

  • 自然形之與氣隨其化用 - Description of the dragon's form and energy transforming naturally.

  • 忽闻鸡鸣神女乃留诗曰 - Indicates that suddenly hearing the rooster's crow, the goddess leaves behind a poem.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 会昌中 - Period of the Tang Dynasty during the reign of Emperor Wuzong (840–846).
  • 因疾退于家 - Literally means 'retired from official duties due to illness,' indicating that the protagonist left his position to rest at home.
  • 忽召所亲自言 - Literally means 'suddenly summoned his family members to speak,' suggesting a moment of revelation or confession.
  • 得疾时 - Refers to the time when the protagonist first became ill.
  • 阴司録君二魂 - Literally means 'the underworld records your two souls,' referring to the belief in multiple souls or spirits in Chinese folk religion.
  • 对事垒一魂主身 - Literally means 'one soul is responsible for the body,' indicating that one soul remains with the body while the other is taken away.
  • 不觉随去 - Literally means 'without realizing, he followed,' suggesting that the protagonist was unaware of being taken away.
  • 出通化门东南入径 - Literally means 'exited through the Tonghua Gate in the southeast and entered a narrow path,' describing the protagonist's journey.
  • 渡灞浐 - Literally means 'crossed the Ba and Chan rivers,' referring to two rivers near Chang'an (modern-day Xi'an).
  • 陟蓝田山 - Literally means 'ascended Mount Lantian,' indicating that the protagonist climbed a mountain.
  • 山上约行数十里 - Literally means 'traveled about ten li on the mountain,' describing the distance covered.
  • 忽见一骑执黑旛 - Literally means 'suddenly saw a rider holding a black flag,' suggesting a supernatural encounter.
  • 太一登殿已久 - Literally means 'the Supreme Being has been on the throne for a long time,' referring to a deity in Chinese mythology.
  • 罪人毕録尔何迟也 - Literally means 'all the guilty have been recorded, why are you late?' indicating that the protagonist was being scolded for being late.
  • 督之而去 - Literally means 'was ordered to leave,' suggesting that the protagonist was forced to continue his journey.
  • 至一城甲士翼门 - Literally means 'arrived at a city with soldiers guarding the gates,' describing the protagonist's arrival at a fortified location.
  • 直北至一宫 - Literally means 'went directly north to a palace,' indicating that the protagonist approached a royal residence.
  • 宫门守卫甚严 - Literally means 'the palace gates were guarded very strictly,' emphasizing the security of the place.
  • 有赤衣吏引使者同入 - Literally means 'a red-cloaked official led the messenger inside,' describing the protagonist's entry into the palace.
  • 萧屏间有一吏自内出 - Literally means 'in a secluded room, an official emerged from inside,' indicating that someone appeared to communicate with the protagonist.
  • 受敎受敎 - Literally means 'received instructions received instructions,' suggesting that the protagonist was given a command or message.
  • 鞠躬受命 - Literally means 'bowed respectfully to receive the order,' indicating that the protagonist complied with the instruction.
  • 宣曰 - Literally means 'declared,' indicating that the protagonist was told something important.
  • 史遂前世括苍山主録大夫 - Literally means 'in a past life, Shi Sui was the chief recorder of Kuocang Mountain,' referring to the protagonist's previous incarnation.
  • 侍者始则恭恪中间废 - Literally means 'the attendant was initially respectful but later became negligent,' indicating a change in behavior over time.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 会昌中 - Refers to the reign of Emperor Wuzong of Tang, a period known for religious persecution and political turmoil.
  • 史遂 - The protagonist's name, which may be a historical or fictional figure.
  • 阴司 - Refers to the underworld, a concept in Chinese folk religion and Buddhism where souls are judged after death.
  • 太一 - A deity in Chinese mythology, often associated with the supreme being or the North Star.
  • 灞浐 - Two rivers near Chang'an, the capital of the Tang Dynasty, often mentioned in classical literature.
  • 蓝田山 - A mountain near Chang'an, known for its scenic beauty and historical significance.
  • 赤衣吏 - Refers to officials dressed in red, a color associated with authority and power in Chinese culture.
  • 萧屏间 - A secluded room, often used in classical literature to describe a private or sacred space.
  • 括苍山 - A mountain in Zhejiang Province, known for its spiritual and historical importance.
  • 主録大夫 - A high-ranking official responsible for recording and managing important documents or affairs.
  • 侍者 - Refers to an attendant or servant, often used in religious or courtly contexts.

Proper Nouns

  • 会昌 - The era name of Emperor Wuzong of Tang.
  • 通化门 - A gate in Chang'an.
  • 灞浐 - Two rivers near Chang'an.
  • 蓝田山 - A mountain near Chang'an.
  • 太一 - A deity in Chinese mythology.
  • 括苍山 - A mountain in Zhejiang Province.
  • 主録大夫 - A high-ranking official.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 堕谪官黄门 - Refers to officials who were demoted to lower positions, specifically eunuchs (黄门) who were often in such roles.

  • 大夫复位 - Refers to officials returning to their original positions.

  • 遂领就一院 - Means 'then led to a courtyard' or 'then brought to a courtyard.'

  • 巂州巂县 - Refers to a specific location, possibly a county in the state of Shu during the Three Kingdoms period.
  • 县令 - Refers to the local magistrate or county official.
  • 蜀马 - Refers to a horse from the state of Shu.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 巨湫渺弥无际 - Vivid phrase meaning 'a vast, boundless lake,' used to describe a large body of water.
  • 张翁夫妇独存 - Straightforward expression meaning 'only the couple of Zhang翁 (a man) and his wife remained alive.'
  • 其后人虵俱失 - Ambiguous phrase; could mean 'their descendants and snakes both disappeared' or 'their descendants and snakes both lost something.'
  • 陷河县曰虵 - Compound phrase meaning 'the county was named Shé (蛇, snake),' indicating a change in the name of the place.
  • 张恶子尔后 - Phrase meaning 'after Zhang恶子 (a person with a negative connotation),' suggesting a continuation of events or a reference to someone named Zhang恶子.
  • 姚苌游蜀至梓潼岭上憩于路傍 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'Yao Chang traveled to Shu and rested by the roadside on the Zitong Ridge.'
  • 有布衣来谓苌曰 - Phrase meaning 'a commoner came and said to Yao Chang.'
  • 君宜早还秦 - Phrase meaning 'you should return to Qin early.'
  • 秦人将无主 - Phrase meaning 'the people of Qin will have no ruler.'
  • 其康济者在君乎 - Phrase meaning 'the one who will bring peace and prosperity is you?'
  • 请其氏曰 - Phrase meaning 'ask for his name and said.'
  • 吾张恶子也 - Phrase meaning 'I am Zhang恶子.'
  • 他日勿相忘 - Phrase meaning 'do not forget me in the future.'
  • 苌还后果称帝于长安 - Phrase meaning 'after Yao Chang returned, he eventually became emperor in Chang'an.'
  • 因命使至蜀求之弗获 - Phrase meaning 'he then ordered an envoy to go to Shu to find him, but he was not found.'
  • 遂立庙于所见之处 - Phrase meaning 'he then built a temple at the place where he saw him.'
  • 今张相公庙是也 - Phrase meaning 'this is the Temple of Zhang相公 (a title given to Zhang恶子).'
  • 僖宗幸蜀日 - Phrase meaning 'when Emperor Xizong visited Shu.'
  • 其神自庙出十余里 - Phrase meaning 'the spirit emerged from the temple and traveled more than ten li.'
  • 列仗迎驾 - Phrase meaning 'formed an array to welcome the emperor.'
  • 白雾之中髣髴见其形 - Phrase meaning 'in the white mist, vaguely saw his form.'
  • 因解佩劒赐之 - Phrase meaning 'then took off his jade sword and gave it to him.'
  • 祝令効顺 - Phrase meaning 'blessed him to be loyal.'
  • 指期贼平 - Phrase meaning 'pointed out the time when the rebels would be defeated.'
  • 驾回广赠珍玩 - Phrase meaning 'when the emperor returned, he widely gave precious objects and playthings.'
  • 人莫敢窥 - Phrase meaning 'no one dared to look at him.'
  • 王铎有诗刋石 - Phrase meaning 'Wang Du had a poem inscribed on stone.'
  • 夜雨龙抛三尺匣 - Poetic phrase meaning 'in the night rain, the dragon threw away the three-foot box.'
  • 春风凤入九重城 - Poetic phrase meaning 'in the spring wind, the phoenix entered the nine-layer city.'

  • 张恶子 - A person with a negative connotation, possibly a historical or mythological figure.

  • 夜雨龙抛三尺匣 - A poetic phrase, possibly referring to a legendary event or symbol.

  • 因命使至蜀求之弗获 - The sentence structure is somewhat complex and could be interpreted in different ways.
  • 遂立庙于所见之处 - The phrase is somewhat elliptical and could be interpreted in different ways.
